jokes.text: Larry discovers his father spelled 'passed' as 'past' on his mother's gravestone to save money
jokes.setup: Larry visits his mother's grave and reads the headstone
jokes.punchline: His father deliberately misspelled 'passed away' as 'past away' to save $100 ($50 per letter)
jokes.explanation: Perfect character setup showing Larry's father's cheap nature creating lasting embarrassment. The specificity of $50 per letter makes it believable and horrifying.

jokes.text: "past away"? P-a-s-t? Dad, you spelled "passed" wrong. It's not spelled p-a-s-t.
jokes.setup: Larry discovers his father's misspelling on his mother's gravestone
jokes.punchline: The realization that his cheapskate father intentionally misspelled it
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry David setup - taking a mundane detail and escalating it. The horror of discovering cheapness at a sacred moment is perfect character work.

jokes.text: Nat defends the misspelling: 'It has the same meaning. Everybody knows what it means.'
jokes.setup: Larry is outraged about the misspelling
jokes.punchline: His father doubles down with flawed logic that misspelling is fine if people understand it
jokes.explanation: Shows the father's stubborn refusal to admit wrongdoing, escalating the cringe factor.

jokes.text: Larry suggests adding 'blandishments' and 'beloved mother of larry' - Nat responds 'You wanted maybe $6,000 more on the stone?'
jokes.setup: Larry wants proper tribute language on the headstone
jokes.punchline: Father reveals the cost would be astronomical, justifying his cheapness
jokes.explanation: Continues the cheapness theme with escalating costs, showing how deep the father's frugality runs.

jokes.text: 'Lucky he didn't put "beloved moth."'
jokes.setup: Continuing discussion about the cheap father's corner-cutting
jokes.punchline: Imagining he'd cut off letters from 'mother' too
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that extends the penny-pinching logic to absurd conclusion. Economical and quotable.

jokes.text: I'd like the same thing... Egg whites with lox and well-done onions. I don't want them limp. I really want them crispy.
jokes.setup: Larry's very specific order for crispy onions
jokes.punchline: The obsessive specificity that will cause problems
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry obsession setup - his pickiness will clearly cause cascading problems later.

jokes.text: No no, it takes a long time to get onions well-done. Believe me. I've ordered this before. I know exactly what I want.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to warn Larry about the time delay
jokes.punchline: Larry's stubborn insistence despite the obvious warning
jokes.explanation: The cringe builds as we see Larry ignoring good advice - classic setup for later payoff.

jokes.text: Larry refuses to introduce his friend Jim: 'He's from New York. You're never gonna see him again.'
jokes.setup: Larry's friends expect an introduction to Jim
jokes.punchline: Larry's practical but rude dismissal of social conventions based on geography
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry logic - technically rational but socially tone-deaf. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Larry dismisses introductions as 'pointless and unnecessary social convention'
jokes.setup: Friends argue introductions are courteous
jokes.punchline: Larry's philosophical rejection of basic social norms
jokes.explanation: Sets up Larry's anti-social convention stance that will be called back later. Pure Larry philosophy.

jokes.text: Larry lies to Mr. Takahashi about his father's 'quadruple bypass surgery yesterday'
jokes.setup: Larry gets caught using his phone in the dining room
jokes.punchline: He creates an elaborate medical emergency lie to justify breaking club rules
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's instinct to lie when caught, creating more problems. The specificity of 'quadruple bypass' is funny.

jokes.text: Mr. Takahashi's dismissive 'You do this all time. Last warning.'
jokes.setup: Larry's elaborate medical excuse
jokes.punchline: Takahashi completely ignores the lie and calls out Larry's pattern
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Larry's dramatic lie. Takahashi's broken English delivery adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: Will you please finish shoveling that shit into your face? It's exactly how I wanted it done.
jokes.setup: Larry's crispy onions causing delays
jokes.punchline: His defensive satisfaction despite causing the exact problem he was warned about
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the onions setup - Larry's stubborn satisfaction in the face of consequences is peak character comedy.

jokes.text: The golf course delay caused by Andy's crispy onions leads to missing their tee time
jokes.setup: Andy insisted on the time-consuming onion preparation
jokes.punchline: The delay causes exactly the problem they warned about - missing their tee time
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback structure - setup pays off exactly as predicted, showing cause and effect.

jokes.text: Larry protests the restaurant's 18% included tip plus additional tip space: 'I have to start doing math in my head now?'
jokes.setup: The bill has built-in gratuity but asks for more
jokes.punchline: Larry's outrage at being forced to calculate additional percentages
jokes.explanation: Relatable annoyance elevated by Larry's specific mathematical complaint. Very quotable premise.

jokes.text: Um, see, 'cause you didn't leave a tip. And usually when I've provided excellent service, my customers like to tip me.
jokes.setup: Larry's principled protest meeting reality
jokes.punchline: The waiter directly confronting Larry's cheap behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - Larry's principles colliding with social reality and service worker confrontation.

jokes.text: Well, there is an 18% tip included. Generally, I do leave an additional tip, but you know what? I'm kind of protesting the additional tip.
jokes.setup: Larry having to explain his protest to the affected worker
jokes.punchline: The awkwardness of explaining principle to someone who just wants their tip
jokes.explanation: The cringe of having to justify your cheapness as principle to someone it directly affects.

jokes.text: Larry and friends argue with slow golfer Norm, leading to escalating insults
jokes.setup: Norm takes 20 minutes looking for a lost ball
jokes.punchline: The confrontation escalates to 'Shove it up your ass' / 'Fuck you, Norm!'
jokes.explanation: Standard golf course conflict. Sets up Norm's heart attack but the argument itself is fairly generic.

jokes.text: Larry sarcastically asks Andy 'Are you happy you had the onions?! Are you glad you had the onions?!'
jokes.setup: The golf delay and fight with Norm
jokes.punchline: Larry blames the entire disaster on Andy's breakfast obsession
jokes.explanation: Strong callback to the onion setup, showing how small decisions create big consequences. Good escalation.

jokes.text: Larry mimics Andy: 'Crispy onions! They have to be crispy! I can't eat breakfast unless I have crispy onions!'
jokes.setup: Continuing to blame Andy for the golf disaster
jokes.punchline: Larry's exaggerated impression of Andy's breakfast obsession
jokes.explanation: Continues the onion callback with Larry's characteristic mock-dramatic delivery.

jokes.text: Club member blames Larry for Norm's heart attack: 'Norm is dead. That's right.'
jokes.setup: Larry yelled at Norm during the golf argument
jokes.punchline: The casual delivery of news that Norm died from the confrontation
jokes.explanation: Dark turn that escalates the consequences. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier and more shocking.

jokes.text: Larry admits 'No, I thought he was a prick' when asked if he liked Norm
jokes.setup: Club member asks if Larry even liked Norm
jokes.punchline: Larry's brutally honest admission about the deceased
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - completely inappropriate honesty at the worst possible time. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: 'The minute you wanted those crispy onions, you put a gun to that guy's head'
jokes.setup: Blaming the entire chain of events on Andy's breakfast choice
jokes.punchline: Connecting the onions directly to Norm's death with violent metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that connects the breakfast scene to the death with dramatic metaphor. Strong cause-and-effect structure.

jokes.text: Larry spontaneously offers to pay for his cousin's daughter's college education
jokes.setup: Discussion about college costs for baby Skylar
jokes.punchline: Larry's impulsive generous offer that surprises everyone
jokes.explanation: Sets up future complications. The gesture is funny because it's both generous and impulsive, typical Larry.

jokes.text: Larry refuses to show Cassie's hats to Jerry Seinfeld: 'Jerry Seinfeld is not gonna wear that hat.' 'You don't know!' 'Yeah, I do know.'
jokes.setup: Cassie wants Larry to promote her hats to celebrities
jokes.punchline: Larry's absolute certainty about Jerry's hat preferences
jokes.explanation: Good back-and-forth showing Larry's blunt realism vs. Cassie's optimism. Meta-humor about Seinfeld.

jokes.text: Stonemason Ed interrupts Larry's call to trash Derek Jeter: 'That guy sucks... He's the most overrated player in baseball'
jokes.setup: Larry is on a business call about his mother's headstone
jokes.punchline: The stonemason derails the conversation with unprompted Yankees criticism
jokes.explanation: Sets up the stonemason as an abrasive character. The sports interruption feels realistic and awkward.

jokes.text: Larry accidentally kills the swan and his friends realize it's Takahashi's beloved pet Kyoko
jokes.setup: Larry was just defending himself from the swan
jokes.punchline: The dead swan turns out to be the club owner's precious pet
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - what seemed like self-defense becomes a major catastrophe. Great story structure.

jokes.text: He attacked me! He leapt at me! You think I wanted to kill it? I didn't want to kill a swan!
jokes.setup: Larry went to retrieve his golf ball near the pond
jokes.punchline: Larry accidentally killed Takahashi's beloved pet swan in self-defense
jokes.explanation: The absurdity of Larry accidentally killing a swan while golfing is perfectly outrageous. His panicked explanation adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: He attacked me! He leapt at me! Did you provoke him? No, I didn't do anything.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining the swan attack
jokes.punchline: Jeff's immediate suspicion that Larry must have provoked it
jokes.explanation: Jeff's knowledge of Larry's character leading him to assume guilt - good character-based comedy.

jokes.text: Jeff's philosophy: 'The only time I tell her I'm playing golf is when I'm with another woman!'
jokes.setup: Discussion about whether to tell wives about the swan incident
jokes.punchline: Jeff's backwards approach to marital honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfectly twisted logic that reveals Jeff's approach to marriage. Dark but brilliantly structured.

jokes.text: Larry's logic: 'Swan killers leave. People who aren't swan killers stay, have lunch, socialize'
jokes.setup: Friends want to leave the club immediately
jokes.punchline: Larry's counterintuitive criminal psychology about appearing innocent
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry logic - technically sound reasoning applied to absurd situation. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Hey, let me remind you of something, asshole: you're talking way too loud about swan killing in the dining room.
jokes.setup: The group arguing about their swan cover-up
jokes.punchline: Larry's practical concern about volume while discussing murder
jokes.explanation: Larry's practical focus on volume control while discussing murder is perfectly absurd character consistency.

jokes.text: Larry argues about fruit consumption: 'You can't have fruit twice in one day. You get the acid. It'll rip a hole in your stomach.'
jokes.setup: Discussion about what to order for lunch
jokes.punchline: Larry's confident but ridiculous nutritional advice
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry pseudo-science delivered with complete confidence. Shows his need to have opinions on everything.

jokes.text: The group panics seeing what they think is a search party but it's just a bird
jokes.setup: The friends are paranoid about being discovered
jokes.punchline: Their guilty consciences make them see threats everywhere
jokes.explanation: Good paranoia gag showing how guilt affects perception. Standard misdirection structure.

jokes.text: Larry justifies killing the swan: 'It was self-defense! What could it have possibly done to you?' 'It could've killed me.' 'It's not a wild boar!'
jokes.setup: Friends question Larry's fear of the swan
jokes.punchline: Larry's dramatic overestimation of swan danger vs. reality check
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Larry's tendency to dramatize. The 'wild boar' comparison is a nice reality check.

jokes.text: It was a bird. What were you so scared of? A bird! It was self-defense! What could it have possibly done to you? It's not a wild boar!
jokes.setup: Takahashi confronting Larry about the swan
jokes.punchline: Larry's dramatic self-defense claim about a bird
jokes.explanation: Larry's absurd escalation of a bird encounter to life-threatening self-defense situation. Good character work.

jokes.text: Mr. Takahashi's interrogation using the tee sheet: 'Only one other group played. Course not crowded. Respect for Norm.'
jokes.setup: Takahashi investigates who could have killed his swan
jokes.punchline: His broken English detective work using golf records
jokes.explanation: Good detective scene with Takahashi's distinctive speech pattern. Creates tension through methodical investigation.

jokes.text: You. You smart guy. You not kill swan. But you know who kill swan. You tell me, free dues. No more dues. For life? Whole life.
jokes.setup: Takahashi interrogating the group
jokes.punchline: The escalating bribe offers for information
jokes.explanation: Takahashi's escalating corruption offers - good interrogation scene structure with increasing stakes.

jokes.text: Takahashi insults Jeff: 'You stupid. Yes. You marry big-mouth wife.' Jeff agrees: 'She does have a big mouth.'
jokes.setup: Takahashi tries psychological pressure on Jeff
jokes.punchline: Jeff immediately agrees with the insult about his wife
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Jeff's spinelessness and willingness to throw his wife under the bus.

jokes.text: Two days in a row. This is worse than playing behind him.
jokes.setup: Memorial service followed by funeral
jokes.punchline: Larry's complaint that dead Norm is still inconveniencing him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark callback - even dead, Norm is still slowing Larry down. Perfect character selfishness.

jokes.text: Larry's outrage: 'I make this generous offer to pay for your daughter's education and now you want me to put your wife through cosmetology school?!'
jokes.setup: Nat's additional request for beauty school tuition
jokes.punchline: Larry's indignant reaction to the scope creep of his generosity
jokes.explanation: Good Larry reaction showing his limits when generosity is taken advantage of. Realistic indignation.

jokes.text: Nat deflects: 'Who knows? She might be a drug addict' about his daughter potentially not going to college
jokes.setup: Larry argues his college offer was generous enough
jokes.punchline: Nat casually suggests his baby daughter might become a drug addict
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and unexpected. Shows Nat's casual pessimism about his own daughter's future.

jokes.text: Nat reveals he told his wife about the swan: 'I had to tell her, Larry. Don't worry. She's not gonna say anything.'
jokes.setup: The group agreed to keep the swan killing secret
jokes.punchline: Nat immediately broke the pact and told his wife
jokes.explanation: Predictable but effective callback to earlier agreement. Shows how secrets inevitably get out.

jokes.text: Larry threatens to destroy Cassie's hat collection: 'The blue hat, the orange hat, the one with the cockle shells, the one with the feathers, and let's not forget the red one you're making for that asshole stonemason'
jokes.setup: Larry is furious about the secret being revealed
jokes.punchline: His specific, detailed threats about destroying each individual hat
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with specific details making the threat funnier. Connects to stonemason callback.

jokes.text: Ed the stonemason's response: 'I hope I get to do yours real soon, prick.'
jokes.setup: Larry just insulted Ed without knowing he was there
jokes.punchline: Ed wishes death on Larry with professional reference to making his headstone
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark comeback connecting his profession to a death wish. Perfect economy and devastating delivery.

jokes.text: I'm an asshole? Who are you? I'm ed... The stonemason. ah.
jokes.setup: Larry defending himself against Jerry's complaints
jokes.punchline: The horrified realization of who he's been talking to
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the introduction theme - Larry's philosophy backfires spectacularly when he needs to know who someone is.

jokes.text: Larry complains Jerry didn't introduce him: 'What about your policy? No introductions.' 'I know, but there's extenuating circumstances.' 'So I'm hoisted on my own petard.'
jokes.setup: Larry realizes Jerry should have warned him about Ed being there
jokes.punchline: Larry's own anti-introduction policy backfired on him spectacularly
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback structure showing consequences of Larry's earlier stance. The 'petard' reference shows his self-awareness.

jokes.text: At Norm's memorial, Larry theorizes the swan killed Norm: 'I believe this is what happened: Norm had the heart attack on the 12th hole near the pond... I think he was attacked by the black swan.'
jokes.setup: Larry is at a solemn memorial service for Norm
jokes.punchline: He creates an elaborate theory blaming the swan for Norm's death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deflection strategy that's both absurd and technically plausible. Great character work showing Larry's self-serving logic.

jokes.text: Larry concludes: 'I don't know who killed that black swan, but whoever did it was a hero. A hero.'
jokes.setup: Larry's theory that the swan killed Norm
jokes.punchline: He declares the swan killer (himself) a hero at Norm's memorial
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry audacity - calling himself a hero for killing the swan at the victim's memorial. Brilliantly inappropriate and self-serving.

jokes.text: Larry gives a huge tip to silence the waiter, abandoning his anti-tipping protest
jokes.setup: Larry has been protesting additional tips all episode
jokes.punchline: He gives a massive tip to buy the waiter's silence about the swan
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic conclusion to the tipping subplot. Shows how principles crumble under pressure.

jokes.text: Mr. Takahashi is convinced by the big tip that Larry couldn't be the swan killer: 'Now I know a man so generous could not have killed my black swan'
jokes.setup: Takahashi has been investigating who killed his swan
jokes.punchline: The bribe money actually convinces him of Larry's innocence
jokes.explanation: Beautiful irony - the cover-up actually provides the perfect alibi. Great resolution showing how corruption can work in unexpected ways.

jokes.text: Larry convinces Takahashi to eliminate the additional tipping by making it a flat 25%: 'Nobody wants to sit and do math after they eat'
jokes.setup: Larry's long-standing complaint about tipping math
jokes.punchline: He successfully reforms the club's tipping policy by making it even more expensive
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback resolution that's both a victory and a defeat for Larry. Gets what he wanted but at higher cost.
